title: Finite Barycentric Interpolation Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite barycentric interpolation bad value QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact rational barycentric interpolation replay for f(x)=x^2 at nodes 0,1,3 computes barycentric_value = 4 at x=2, while a malformed row claims barycentric_value = 5, isolating a compact scalar equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional interpolation rows only when they add distinct stability, node-ordering, barycentric-update, spline, floating-point, or theorem-reconstruction pressure; keep general interpolation error and conditioning theory in future Lean or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed polynomial, nodes, sample values, barycentric weights, numerator terms, denominator terms, node-hit data, and interpolation value as deterministic rational replay obligations.
Route the false barycentric interpolation value through checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence after replay computes the exact value.
Expose the pack through polynomial, calculus, real-analysis, numerical-analysis, and learner query surfaces.

Keep general interpolation uniqueness, barycentric/Lagrange/Newton equivalence, error estimates, conditioning, Runge phenomena, spline theory, floating-point interpolation correctness, and numerical-stability claims marked as future Lean or numerical-honesty work until formal routes exist.
